subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: triangulated across multilateral bodies (IMF Article IV May-2026, OECD, Eurostat, RSF, Transparency International, Venice Commission) + Spanish official sources (INE, Banco de España, AIReF, Ministerio del Interior) + Spanish/Catalan independent press (El País, elDiario, elEspañol, Catalan News).
Source-lean: mild Anglophone amplification on the NATO-5% and anti-tourism stories; Spanish-language sources confirmed the underlying facts with less alarm. Slant balance adequate.
Climate (water stress + fast warming) and a fragile minority government are the standout long-term drags.

economic.notes: A strong growth story - record tourism, a widening current-account surplus, sound banks - within the eurozone; a high (if falling) debt load is the structural drag.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt ~100.6% GDP (falling toward ~99%); deficit ~2.4%, a sixth straight beat and a first primary surplus since 2007; but ageing-cost pressure and no budget passed since 2023. (IMF Art IV May-2026, Banco de España, AIReF)

monetary_independence.notes: Eurozone - ECB-set, credible and independent, no domestic interference. (ECB)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Eurozone member; the euro is the #2 global reserve currency, a modest shared benefit. (IMF COFER)

inflation.notes: HICP projected up to ~3% end-2026 on an energy shock, back to ~2.2% in 2027; near-term overshoot, well-managed under the ECB. (IMF, EC, Banco de España)

banking_stability.notes: CET1 ~13.5%, ROE ~14.1%, NPL ~2.9% and falling; BBVA-Sabadell consolidation; a windfall tax is a margin drag, not a stability risk. (Banco de España FSR, IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Record tourism (~97M arrivals) and a widening current-account surplus with strong non-tourism services exports; stable trade policy; no resource curse; an anti-tourism backlash is a mild long-term cap. (INE, CaixaBank/BBVA Research)

institutional.notes: Solid courts and independent statistics, eroding on judicial-appointment politicization and press pressure.

rule_of_law.notes: Functioning courts and property rights, but a contested amnesty law (upheld 6-4 by the Constitutional Court), a politicized judicial-appointment process flagged by the Venice Commission, and lawfare around corruption probes. (EU Rule of Law Report 2025, Venice Commission)

statistical_integrity.notes: INE is independent and Eurostat-compliant; AIReF is a credible fiscal watchdog; no documented interference. (Eurostat, AIReF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Competent professional bureaucracy; regional fragmentation and primary-care/registry staffing gaps. (OECD, WHO)

press_freedom.notes: RSF 23rd (2025) slipping to 29th (2026); SLAPPs, political pressure, and high ownership concentration. (RSF)

political_social.notes: The weakest category: a fragile minority government and a housing/cohesion strain, against strong civil liberties.

political_stability.notes: A PSOE minority that lost a key partner's support, no budget since 2023, serial corruption probes, and early-election pressure; institutions remain peaceful. (El País, Freedom House)

civil_liberties.notes: Strong speech/assembly/due process; a 'gag law' reform pending; protest space robust. (Freedom House, V-Dem)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Lawful, durable pathways (non-lucrative visa, the 2023 digital-nomad visa, EU Blue Card); citizenship after 10 years (2 for Ibero-Americans); the Golden Visa was abolished April 2025, narrowing the wealth route, but core residency paths and 2025 regularization momentum remain. (immigration bar, gov sources)

social_cohesion.notes: Catalan secession tension eased materially post-amnesty, but a housing-affordability crisis and anti-tourism protests are the new fault line. (Catalan News, Euronews)

demographics.notes: Fertility ~1.2 (among the world's lowest); 65+ heading toward ~30% by 2055; population still grows only via immigration; near-term migration cushions, long-term ageing is locked in. (INE projections, Elcano)

geopolitical.notes: NATO/EU anchored and geographically insulated; alliance reliability dinged by the defense-spending refusal.

alliance_reliability.notes: A committed NATO/EU member, but the only ally to refuse the 5% target (capped ~2.1%), drawing US retaliation threats and some sidelining from key Ukraine talks - a credibility ding, not a rupture. (Atlantic Council, NATO)

conflict_involvement.notes: No active conflict; geographically insulated; only African-coast migration friction. (multilateral)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: EU sanctions regime; no capital controls; full financial integration. (EU)

physical_practical.notes: Strong healthcare, low crime, and a functioning catastrophe-insurance backstop, against acute climate/water stress and a grid-reliability flag.

climate.notes: Warming ~1.6x the global mean, structural water stress/desertification, extreme heat, and wildfire; high readiness cannot offset physical drying. (AEMET; IPCC AR6; EEA)

healthcare.notes: Universal coverage, low preventable/treatable mortality, high system trust, long life expectancy (~84); a primary-care staffing deficit and regional strain. (OECD/EU Health Profile 2025, WHO)

infrastructure.notes: Excellent high-speed rail, ports, and broadband; but the April-2025 nationwide blackout and grid-investment lag are real reliability flags. (INE, The Conversation)

crime_safety.notes: Homicide ~0.64/100k (among Europe's lowest), stable/falling; petty theft concentrated in tourist cities. (Ministerio del Interior, Eurostat)

disaster_insurance.notes: The Oct-2024 DANA (Valencia, EUR 20B+ losses) was the public Consorcio's largest-ever event yet it functioned, paying claims at scale - a genuine mutualized backstop against rising flood risk. (Consorcio de Compensación de Seguros, S&P)

summary: A standout growth story - record tourism, a current-account surplus, sound banks, low crime, good healthcare - within the eurozone, with lawful residency pathways. The drags are a high (if falling) debt load, a fragile minority government dogged by corruption probes and unable to pass a budget, rule-of-law politicization around judicial appointments and the amnesty law, and a structurally water-stressed, fast-warming climate with locked-in long-run ageing.
